meta.title: Korean Labor Standards Calculators — Workmate
meta.description: Annual leave, weekly rest pay, severance, minimum wage — calculators based on Korea's Labor Standards Act with step-by-step explanations and legal citations.

laborCalcHub.heading: Tenure, leave, and pay in one place
laborCalcHub.intro: Built on Korea's Labor Standards Act §55 and §60 plus the Severance Act. Each tool shows the inputs and the legal calculation step by step.

annual-leave.title: Annual Leave Calculator
annual-leave.description: Enter your hire date — get accrued leave days plus unused-leave compensation. Handles sub-1-year monthly accrual and 1-year-plus increments.

weekly-rest-pay.title: Weekly Rest Pay
weekly-rest-pay.description: For employees working 15+ hours per week. Pro-rated short-time worker calculation coming soon.

severance.title: Severance Pay
severance.description: After 1+ year tenure: 30 days of average wage × years served. Auto 3-month average calculation coming soon.

min-wage-monthly.title: Min Wage → Monthly
min-wage-monthly.description: 2026 minimum wage at 209 hours plus annual salary and net pay comparison. With/without weekly rest pay split coming soon.

meta.title: Korean Severance Pay Calculator (Avg vs Ordinary Wage)
meta.description: Hire date, resign date, last 3 months' salary → average daily wage + ordinary wage comparison → severance auto. Optional bonus + unused-leave additions.

severanceTool.heading: Severance Pay Calculator
severanceTool.intro: Average daily wage × 30 × (service days ÷ 365). Higher of average or ordinary daily wage applies. Optional 3/12 portion of annual bonus and unused-leave pay.

hints.recentThreeMonthsSalary: Base + fixed allowances summed over 3 months
hints.annualBonus: Regular and definite bonuses only (3/12 added)
hints.annualLeavePay: Last 1 year's unused leave pay (3/12 added)
hints.monthlyOrdinaryWage: Used if ordinary daily wage > average

result.heading: Result
result.empty: Enter dates and wages.
result.notQualifiedBody: Tenure {days} days — under 365 days = no severance (Worker Retirement Benefits Act §4).

steps.heading: Steps
steps.averageWage: Average = (3-month wage {threeMonthSalary} + bonus 3/12 {bonusPortion} + leave 3/12 {leavePayPortion}) ÷ 90 days = daily {averageDailyWage}

errors.unknown: An error occurred during calculation.

severanceTool.source: Worker Retirement Benefits Act §8 (severance system). Average wage calculation can vary by company and industry. Some companies pay severance for under-1-year service.

law.title: Eligibility
law.body: 1+ year continuous service AND average 15+ hours per week (4-week avg). Triggered regardless of voluntary or involuntary termination. Applies to workplaces under 5 too (since 2010-12-01).

average.title: Average wage
average.body: Last 3 months' total wage ÷ days in that period (typically 90). Regular bonus = 1/4 of annual; unused-leave pay = 1/4 of last year's, both added in.

dc.title: DC vs DB
dc.body: DC (defined contribution): 1/12 monthly wage accumulated each year. This calculator assumes DB (defined benefit). DC final amount depends on investment returns.

validation.salaryPositive: Please enter the last 3 months' wage.
validation.hireBeforeResign: Hire date must be before resign date.

meta.title: Weekly Rest Pay Calculator (Korea, LSA §55)
meta.description: Enter hourly or monthly wage → weekly, monthly, annual rest pay. Part-time prorated (15+ hours/week) automatically per Labor Standards Act §55.

weeklyRestPayTool.heading: Weekly Rest Pay Calculator
weeklyRestPayTool.intro: Paid weekly rest day pay for workers attending all scheduled days. Under 15 hours/week = no rest pay; 15-40 hours = prorated. Hourly or monthly input.

result.heading: Result
result.empty: Enter wage and weekly hours.
result.notQualifiedBody: Weekly {hours}h — under 15h/week is not eligible for weekly rest pay (LSA Enforcement Decree §30).

errors.unknown: An error occurred during calculation.

weeklyRestPayTool.source: Labor Standards Act §55, Enforcement Decree §30. Company rules and collective agreements may differ.

law.title: LSA §55
law.body: Workers attending all scheduled work days in a week receive 1 paid rest day. 'Full attendance' = no absences. Holidays don't count as absences.

formula.title: Part-time proration
formula.body: Part-time rest pay = (weekly hours ÷ 40) × 8 hours × hourly wage. Capped at 8 hours. 30h/week = 6h pay, 20h/week = 4h pay.

exception.title: Not eligible
exception.body: Under 15 hours/week, weeks without full attendance, daily-hire workers (in principle). Applies to workplaces with under 5 employees too. Employer-caused absences may count as attendance.

validation.wageRequired: Please enter hourly or monthly wage.
validation.weeklyHoursPositive: Weekly hours must be greater than 0.

meta.title: Hourly → Monthly Salary Converter (Korea, with weekly rest)
meta.description: Convert hourly wage to monthly salary and annual income using Korea's standard 209-hour basis (40-hour week + weekly rest pay). Part-time prorated.

minWageTool.heading: Hourly → Monthly / Annual
minWageTool.intro: Convert hourly wage to monthly salary using Korea's 209-hour standard (40h/week + weekly rest pay). Part-time hours auto-prorated.

hints.hourlyWage: 0 = use min wage for selected year
hints.includeWeeklyRest: Workers with 15+ weekly hours qualify for weekly rest pay

result.heading: Result
result.empty: Enter hourly wage and weekly hours, then convert.

errors.unknown: An error occurred during conversion.

minWageTool.source: Labor Standards Act §55 (weekly rest day). 209 hours is the administrative standard for monthly working hours. Actual pay may differ per company rules.

formula.title: Where does 209 hours come from?
formula.body: 40 weekly work hours + 8 weekly-rest hours = 48 weekly. 52 weeks ÷ 12 ≈ 4.345 weeks/month. 48 × 4.345 ≈ 208.56 → administratively rounded to 209.

partTime.title: Part-time proration
partTime.body: Under 15 weekly hours → no weekly rest pay. 15-40 hours → weekly rest pay = (weekly hours / 5) hours per week, capped at 8.

minWage.title: Minimum wage decision
minWage.body: Announced yearly by Aug 5 for the following year. 2025 = 10,030 KRW. 2026 TBD — defaults to 2025 value until announced.

meta.title: Korean VAT Calculator — Extract & Compute
meta.description: Split tax-inclusive prices into supply + VAT, add VAT to ex-tax amounts, compute filing tax for general taxpayers, and apply industry-specific value-added rates for simplified taxpayers.

vatTool.heading: VAT Calculator
vatTool.intro: Extract supply value and VAT from a tax-inclusive price, or compute filing tax for general / simplified Korean taxpayers — including industry value-added rates.

amount.add: Pre-VAT supply value to add VAT to

result.heading: Result
result.empty: Enter an amount and click Calculate.

errors.unknown: An error occurred during calculation.

vatTool.source: Source: VAT Act §30 (10% rate), §63 (simplified taxpayer rates). For reference only — actual filing requires deductible input verification and exempt-sales segregation.

law.title: VAT Act §30
law.body: 10% on supply of goods and services. Supply value = total ÷ 1.1. Businesses file by netting sales tax against input tax.

simple.title: Simplified taxpayer
simple.body: Prior-year sales under KRW 104M. Industry value-added rate (15-40%) is applied, then 10% VAT. No mandatory tax invoice issuance.

deadline.title: Filing deadlines
deadline.body: General: H1 (Jan-Jun) by Jul 25, H2 (Jul-Dec) by Jan 25 next year. Simplified: once a year by Jan 25. Provisional filings due quarterly on the 25th.

meta.title: Annual Leave Calculator — Korea (LSA §60)
meta.description: Enter your hire date and salary — get accrued annual leave days and unused-leave compensation per Korea's Labor Standards Act §60. Handles sub-1-year accrual and 25-day cap.

annualLeaveTool.heading: Annual Leave Calculator
annualLeaveTool.intro: Hire date, salary, unused days — get accrued leave and unused-leave compensation in one go. Sub-1-year monthly accrual and 2-year increments are auto-handled.

hints.referenceDate: Defaults to today. Use for forward simulation.
hints.dailyOrdinaryWage: If you've already calculated daily wage
hints.unusedDays: Days to convert into compensation (0 = accrual only)

result.heading: Result
result.empty: Enter your hire date and click Calculate.

steps.heading: Steps
steps.monthlyAccrual: Sub-1-year — 1 day per month worked: {totalMonths} months → {daysAccrued} days (cap {cap})
steps.annualIncrement: Increment — +1 day every {interval} years, {increments} applied → {total} days total
steps.wageDerive: Monthly wage {monthly} KRW ÷ 209 × 8 = daily wage {daily} KRW
steps.lowAttendance: Attendance < 80% — only 1 day per fully-attended month (LSA §60 ②)

warnings.heading: Notes
warnings.futureHireDate: Hire date is in the future. Treat the result as a simulation.
warnings.lowAttendance: Attendance < 80% accrues only 1 day per fully-attended month. Confirm with HR for precise numbers.
warnings.missingWage: Enter monthly or daily wage for compensation calculation.
warnings.negativeUnused: Unused days must be 0 or greater.

errors.unknown: An error occurred during calculation.

annualLeaveTool.source: Source: Labor Standards Act §60 (Annual Paid Leave). Daily ordinary wage = monthly ÷ 209 × 8 per administrative interpretation. For reference only — collective agreements and work rules at your workplace take precedence.

law.title: LSA §60
law.body: 15 days of paid leave per year for ≥ 80% attendance. Sub-1-year or < 80% gets 1 day per fully-attended month. From year 3, +1 day every 2 years; cap 25 days.

compensation.title: Unused-leave pay
compensation.body: Unused days are paid as daily ordinary wage × unused count. Daily wage = monthly ÷ 209 × 8 (base + fixed allowances).

exception.title: Workplaces under 5
exception.body: Workplaces with fewer than 5 regular employees are exempt from annual leave provisions (LSA §11). Collective agreements may override.

validation.hireAfterReference: Hire date cannot be later than reference date.
validation.unusedDaysNegative: Unused days must be 0 or greater.

projects.heading: Games · Experiences · Services
projects.intro: Side projects running alongside Workmate. A card game, political text adventure, workplace culture audit, academy SaaS — five works built solo, full-stack.
projects.philo1Title: Build what's missing on the ground
projects.philo1Body: Electric calculators, academy SaaS, hwatu card game — all born from hitting limits in existing products. When the user builds it, the details are different.
projects.philo2Body: KEC, KS, NDS, Korean building code, 4 major insurances, hwatu rules — global products miss the local details. Translating foreign sites doesn't work.
projects.philo3Title: Solo full-stack in the AI era
projects.philo3Body: Flutter, Next.js, Supabase, Gemini, Flame — running modern stacks in small units, validating and iterating faster than larger teams can.

meta.title: Breaker Capacity Calculator | Workmate
meta.description: Auto-select MCB/MCCB rated current from load current + safety factor with inverter+ELB warning.

breakerTool.title: Breaker Capacity Calculator
breakerTool.subtitle: Load current × safety factor → smallest KS-standard rating that satisfies the requirement. Includes inverter+ELB nuisance-trip warning.

hints.loadType: Defaults: General 1.25 / Motor direct 1.5 / Y-Δ 2.0 / Inverter 1.25 / Welder 3.0
hints.customSafetyFactor: Leave blank to use the load-type default
hints.useElb: If ELB is used — combined with inverter loads, leakage current can cause nuisance trips

result.heading: Result
result.empty: Enter load current and type, then click Calculate.

source.line2: Note: Inverter + 30mA ELB combination is prone to nuisance tripping due to leakage. MCB/MCCB recommended.

warnings.inverterWithElb: Inverter + ELB combination often causes nuisance tripping due to Y-capacitor leakage. Consider MCB/MCCB instead.
warnings.marginLow: Safety margin is below {threshold}%. Consider stepping up one size.
warnings.highCurrentMcb: Reaching MCB max rating (63A). Larger loads will use MCCB.

validation.loadCurrentPositive: Load current must be greater than 0
validation.loadCurrentMax: Load current must be at most 5000A

errors.noStandardMatch: Required rating exceeds KS standard range.
errors.unknown: An unknown error occurred during calculation.

meta.description: Compute voltage drop and check against Korean Electrical Code distance-based limits (3-7%).

voltageDropTool.title: Voltage Drop Calculator
voltageDropTool.subtitle: Korean Electrical Code formula e = (k × L × I) / (1000 × A) with auto distance-based limit comparison (3% / 5% / 6% / 7%).

result.heading: Result
result.empty: Enter circuit information and click Calculate.

source.line1: Source: Korean Electrical Code (Naesun) 1410-1 — voltage-drop formula and distance limits

errors.unknown: An unknown error occurred during calculation.

insuranceTool.subtitle: 2026 rates. Computes employee + employer contributions and net salary in one pass. Industrial accident rate is industry-specific (editable).

hints.monthlySalary: Pre-tax monthly salary. Excludes non-taxable allowances.
hints.industrialAccidentRate: All-industry average 0.86% (0.0086). Adjust per actual industry.

result.heading: Result
result.empty: Enter monthly salary and click Calculate.

source.line1: Source: NPS · NHIS · Workers' Comp Service · EI (2026 rates)
source.line2: Long-term care: 12.95% of health insurance / Employment Stability: 0.25% (employer only, < 150 employees)
source.line3: Note: Industrial accident rate varies 0.6–30% by industry. Verify with Workers' Comp Service.

warnings.pensionCapped: Salary exceeds the National Pension upper base ({cap} KRW). Capped value used.
warnings.pensionFloored: Salary below the National Pension lower base ({floor} KRW). Floor value used.

validation.salaryMin: Monthly salary must be at least 100,000 KRW
validation.salaryMax: Monthly salary must be at most 100,000,000 KRW

errors.unknown: An unknown error occurred during calculation.

meta.title: Foreign Stock Capital Gains Tax | Workmate
meta.description: Korean residents' foreign stock capital gains tax with FX, KRW 2.5M deduction, 22% rate (income + local).

foreignStockTaxTool.title: Foreign Stock Capital Gains Tax Calculator
foreignStockTaxTool.subtitle: For Korean residents. Applies separate buy/sell exchange rates, KRW 2.5M annual deduction, and 22% (income 20% + local 2%) tax rate.

hints.exchangeRate: Bank of Korea reference rate on the trade date
hints.transactionCost: Brokerage fees and other costs (excluding tax)
hints.prevDeduction: Deduction already used in other trades this year. Subtracted from KRW 2.5M.

result.heading: Result
result.empty: Enter purchase and sale information then click Calculate.

source.line1: Source: Income Tax Act §118-2 (Foreign stock capital gains) — 22% inclusive of local tax
source.line2: Annual deduction: KRW 2.5M / Trade-date FX applies / Loss netting allowed (this tool: single trade)
source.line3: Note: Filing in May the following year. This tool is for advance simulation.

steps.gain: Step 3: Capital gain — sell {sell} - buy {buy} - cost {cost} = {result} KRW
steps.deduction: Step 4: Remaining deduction — annual {annual} - used {used} = {remaining} KRW
steps.taxableBase: Step 5: Taxable base — max(0, gain {gain} - deduction {deduction}) = {result} KRW
steps.netProfit: Step 7: Net profit — gain {gain} - tax {tax} = {result} KRW

validation.exchangeRatePositive: Exchange rate must be greater than 0

errors.unknown: An unknown error occurred during calculation.

result.note: Data is processed in your browser only — never sent to a server.

meta.title: Business Number Validation (Checksum) | Workmate
meta.description: Offline format and checksum validation of Korean business registration numbers per the NTS standard algorithm.

bizNumberTool.title: Business Number Validation
bizNumberTool.subtitle: Validates Korean business registration numbers offline using the National Tax Service standard checksum algorithm. Auto-classifies entity type by middle digits. Operating status requires external API.

result.heading: Validation Result
result.empty: Enter a business registration number and click Validate.

source.line2: 9th digit: tens-place carry from ×5 multiplication is added.
source.line3: Note: Format/checksum is 1st-tier validation. Active/closed status requires Hometax API.

steps.extraAdd: Step 4: 9th-digit carry — tens place of {ninth}×5 → {extra} added → {newSum}

warnings.checksumFailed: Checksum mismatch — computed {expected} vs entered {actual}. Likely typo.
warnings.needsExternalApi: Checksum passed, but actual operating/closed status must be checked via the NTS Hometax API.

errors.unknown: Validation error occurred.

meta.title: Wire Size Calculator | Workmate
meta.description: KS C IEC 60364-5-52 wire size calculator with ambient temperature derating, grouping factor, and PVC/XLPE insulation selection.

wireSizeTool.title: Wire Size Calculator
wireSizeTool.subtitle: Selects the smallest KS-standard cross-section satisfying voltage drop, ampacity, ambient temperature derating (Ct), grouping factor (Cg), and insulation type (PVC/XLPE).

hints.insulation: PVC = 70°C conductor / XLPE·EPR = 90°C conductor (XLPE allows ~1.25× ampacity over PVC)
hints.ambientTemperature: Default 30°C. For boiler rooms or outdoor enclosures, enter measured value. Auto-corrected per KS Table B.52.14.
hints.numberOfCircuits: Number of loaded circuits sharing the same conduit/tray (including this one). Auto-corrected per KS Table B.52.17.

result.heading: Result
result.empty: Enter the values and click Calculate.

source.line2: Source: KS C IEC 60364-5-52 Annex B (Ampacity · Method A1)
source.line3: Source: KS C IEC 60364-5-52 Table B.52.14 (Temperature), Table B.52.17 (Grouping)

steps.minSection: Step 2: Minimum cross-section by drop — A_min = ({coefficient} × {distance} × {current}) / (1000 × {drop}) = {result}mm²
steps.derating: Step 3: Derating factors — {insulation} insulation, ambient {ambient}°C → Ct={ct}, {circuits} circuits → Cg={cg} (total {total})
steps.selected: Step 5: KS standard cross-section — both drop (≥{minSection}mm²) and ampacity (base ≥{requiredAmpacity}A) satisfied → {chosen}mm² ({material} {insulation} table {baseAmpacity}A)

warnings.aluminumMinSize: Aluminum conductors are typically not used below {min}mm² (KEC). Consider switching to copper.
warnings.safetyMarginLow: Safety margin is below {threshold}%. Consider stepping up one size to allow for future load growth.
warnings.parallelRecommended: Cross-section {chosen}mm² is impractical as a single cable. Consider parallel circuits (2 or more in parallel).

errors.noStandardMatch: No KS standard cross-section satisfies both ampacity and voltage drop. Review your inputs (ambient/circuits/conductor) or consider parallel cabling.
errors.unknown: An unknown error occurred during calculation.

validation.allowedDropMin: Allowable voltage drop must be at least 0.5%
validation.allowedDropMax: Allowable voltage drop must be at most 10%
validation.ambientMin: Ambient temperature must be at least -10°C
validation.ambientMax: Ambient temperature must be at most 80°C
validation.circuitsInt: Number of circuits must be an integer
validation.circuitsMin: Number of circuits must be at least 1
validation.circuitsMax: Number of circuits must be at most 20

source.line2: Source: Evacuation/Fireproof Structure Rules Article 15 (Riser/Tread limits)

warnings.riserTooHigh: Actual riser {actual}mm exceeds the safety limit of {max}mm. Add more steps.
warnings.treadTooSmall: Tread {actual}mm is below the minimum {min}mm. Increase total run.
warnings.blondelOutOfRange: Blondel value {value}mm is outside the target {min}–{max}mm range. Walking comfort is reduced.
warnings.preferredRiserExceedsLimit: Preferred riser {preferred}mm exceeds the safety limit of {max}mm.

meta.title: Span Calculator | Workmate
meta.description: Maximum span for joists, rafters, and headers via bending + deflection check, per KDS 41 33 02 and NDS 2018.

spanTool.title: Span Calculator
spanTool.subtitle: Section (2x4–2x12) × spacing (400/450/600mm) × species + Korean snow loads → maximum span satisfying both bending and deflection.

hints.snowRegion: Per KDS 41 10 15 Annex B. Yeongdong & Ulleung are 3.0 kN/m² (very high).

result.heading: Result
result.empty: Enter member info and click Calculate.

source.line3: Note: Residential planning tool. Structural engineer review required before construction.

steps.loads: Step 1: Loads — Live {live} + Dead {dead} + Snow {snow} = {total} kN/m²
steps.section: Step 3: Section — {b}×{d}mm → I = {I} mm⁴, S = {S} mm³
steps.fbAdjusted: Step 4: Allowable Fb — {base} MPa × Cr {cr} = {result} MPa
steps.governing: Step 7: Governing — bending {bending}mm vs deflection {deflection}mm → {result}mm ({check} governs)

warnings.rafterNeedsSnow: For rafters, regional snow load is critical. Confirm correct region selected.

errors.noLumberData: Cannot find data for the selected lumber size.
errors.unknown: An unknown error occurred during calculation.

meta.title: Insulation R/U-value Calculator | Workmate
meta.description: Multi-layer wall composite R-value and U-value with automatic pass/fail per Korean Energy Saving Design Code Annex 1.

insulationTool.title: Insulation R/U-value Calculator
insulationTool.subtitle: Region (Central1·Central2·South·Jeju) × element (wall/roof/floor) × layered build-up → composite R and U value with auto code compliance check.

result.heading: Result
result.empty: Set region, element, and layers then click Calculate.

source.line1: Source: Building Energy Saving Design Code Annex 1 (regional U-limits)
source.line2: Source: KS L 9016 (insulation conductivity) / PHIKO references
source.line3: Note: Thermal bridging not modeled. Actual performance depends on installation quality.

steps.layerSum: Step 1: Sum of layer R — {count} layers totaling {sum} m²·K/W
steps.surfaceR: Step 2: Surface resistance — interior {interior} + exterior {exterior} = {total} m²·K/W
steps.totalR: Step 3: Total R — layers {layers} + surface {surface} = {result} m²·K/W

errors.unknown: An unknown error occurred during calculation.

meta.title: Material Quantity Calculator | Workmate
meta.description: From installation area and waste factor, compute sheets of OSB, plywood, gypsum, siding plus nail/screw estimate.

materialQuantityTool.title: Material Quantity Calculator
materialQuantityTool.subtitle: Wall/roof/floor area × (1 + waste factor) ÷ sheet area → sheet count + nail/screw estimate.

hints.estimateFasteners: Estimate edge + field nails/screws for sheet products

result.heading: Result
result.empty: Enter material and area.

source.line1: Source: Korean market standard sizes + IRC R602.3 nailing schedule
source.line2: Note: Fastener counts are estimates assuming 150mm edge / 300mm field spacing.

validation.wasteMin: Waste factor must be at least 0%
validation.wasteMax: Waste factor must be at most 30%

errors.unknown: An unknown error occurred during calculation.

meta.title: Roof Area Calculator | Workmate
meta.description: From plan area and slope angle, compute the actual sloped roof area plus eave area.

roofAreaTool.title: Roof Area Calculator
roofAreaTool.subtitle: Plan area × sec(slope) → actual roof area. Add eave perimeter and overhang to include eave area.

hints.planArea: One-story footprint area covered by the roof
hints.perimeter: Length of eave perimeter. 0 = skip eave area.

result.heading: Result
result.empty: Enter plan area and slope angle.

source.line1: Source: sec(θ) right-triangle correction (plan → slope area)

steps.eaveArea: Step 3: Eave area — perimeter {perimeter}m × overhang {eaveOverhang}mm × sec(θ) {sec} = {result}m²

validation.planAreaMin: Plan area must be at least 1m²
validation.planAreaMax: Plan area must be at most 10000m²
validation.eaveMax: Eave overhang must be at most 2000mm

errors.unknown: An unknown error occurred during calculation.

meta.title: Concrete Volume Calculator | Workmate
meta.description: Strip footing or mat slab concrete volume + rebar weight + remicon truck count.

concreteTool.title: Concrete Volume Calculator
concreteTool.subtitle: Foundation type × dimensions + waste factor → concrete volume + rebar weight + remicon truck count.

hints.rebar: Residential strip footing 80–100 kg/m³, mat 60–80 kg/m³

result.heading: Result
result.empty: Enter foundation dimensions.

source.line1: Source: KDS 14 20 (Concrete Structure Design Code) + Korean remicon standard (6m³/truck)
source.line2: Note: Rebar estimate is residential average. Actual quantity should be derived from structural drawings.

validation.rebarMax: Rebar density must be at most 300 kg/m³
validation.wasteMax: Waste factor must be at most 30%

errors.unknown: An unknown error occurred during calculation.

meta.title: Lumber Conversion | Workmate
meta.description: Convert lumber dimensions and quantity into volume (m³), board feet (BF), sai (才), and estimated weight.

lumberTool.title: Lumber Conversion
lumberTool.subtitle: Cross-section (W×T) × length × quantity × species → volume in m³, sai (才), board feet (BF), and estimated weight all at once.

result.heading: Result
result.empty: Enter member dimensions and quantity.

source.line2: Source: 1 sai = 1 chi × 1 chi × 12 ja ≈ 0.003342 m³
source.line3: Densities are 12% moisture-content averages (actual may vary)

errors.unknown: An unknown error occurred during calculation.
